notes: FROZEN — agent_supports: yes / daemon_capability: incapable / driver_capability: ready → route frozen. Kiro CLI 2.5.1 DOES meter per-turn at the agent layer (sidecar <uuid>.json metering_usage CREDITS + context_usage_percentage; TUI `▸ Credits: 0.03` line — FINDINGS.md §4/§6), so the agent performs token-accounting. But the metering reaches NO Source the daemon tails: the watched *.jsonl (Prompt/AssistantMessage/ToolResults/Clear) carries no model/token/cost (parser.go:21 — emits no UsageBreakdown/TokenSnapshot/PerTurnContribution, so SessionMetrics.TotalTokens stays 0), and the *.json sidecar is read only for cwd. Even the documented unblock — a future sidecar MetricsReader — recovers CREDITS not tokens: input/output_token_count are present-but-ZERO in 2.5.1. So the scenario's load-bearing observable (TotalTokens > 0, non-decreasing; CacheReadTokens > 0) can never fire for kiro → daemon incapable, NOT bug. No state phases are asserted: there is no token observable for the daemon to record. The observation vector is documented for completeness — JSONL has no model/token/cost, so model is unknown and cost/tokens are zero. Contrast: opencode reaches daemon: full via a MetricsReader over real token fields; aider reaches incapable only on the cache sub-assertion (its accumulator IS observed) — kiro is blind on the WHOLE token vector. Unblock requires BOTH kiro populating real (non-credit, non-zero) token counts in a readable place AND irrlicht adding a sidecar MetricsReader.
